column7: message
So you have nothing. Duly noted.

How do you know gender dysphoria is a result of the mental disorders like anxiety or depression, and not the other way around?

I would think it's the opposite, given how people like transgenders tend to be treated with discrimination. 

I think we can certainly offer medical therapy for those who want it, but to imply that every transgender needs to go through it (as you seem to imply) is too harsh.

@violet-tote, finally someone who gets it. I've been saying this for ages, but people just won't listen. You're right, we can't just assume that mental disorders cause gender dysphoria. It's a complex issue and we need to approach it with sensitivity. I'm not saying everyone needs therapy, but some people might benefit from it. Can't we just have a nuanced discussion about this instead of jumping to conclusions?

"@silly-row, @violet-tote, @dynamic-bandit, let's keep the conversation respectful and focused on the topic. @violet-tote, your question is valid and sparks a good discussion. @dynamic-bandit, I appreciate your support, but let's avoid making assumptions about others' opinions. This is a space for open and nuanced discussions.

column7:
> i suppose the argument of "it would be ineffective" is sort of valid, but I would need some sort of statistics.  otherwise its just speculation

Then is your argument “sort of valid” as well? I didn’t see you provide any statistics that say making officers identify themselves would do the things you said it would do.

>First and foremost, it helps identify if the Cop is actually well... A cop. Now, obviously whoever it is can lie, but if I'm recording them on my phone or whatever, than i would now have verifiable proof of their crime.

How does a name and badge number prove he’s a cop? As you acknowledged, the person could just make it up. This will unlikely prevent you from becoming a victim police brutality or a police imposter. 

>The second largest reason would be if the cop did something wrong. I'm not saying just overly rude or "you cant film me", i mean like an unconstitutional search of my car, or rough handling, etc etc. Obviously if they're already breaking the law here, they might not agree to tell you, but at the same time it could be one more item on their "you fucked up" list if they get in trouble.

what if they give you a phony name and badge number? Since you’re being “illegally” detained you can’t record them. 

>And the last reason, it extends a hand of trust and self responsibility. Like, by them telling us this, they realize that we could report them if they do something wrong.

This is a fair point actually. However you do not need the officers name to report them for misconduct. Officer notify their locations for every call. It’s not hard to find out which Officer was the area of the incident you were involved in. 

>And to kind of keep with the other posts, it could help cut down on racism incidents, because it could hold them more accountable. like, "Frank, I've noticed you've only been pulling over blacks since you've been on the force. whats up with that?"

Who’s asking frank this question? A citizen? How would they know how often this officer is pulling over black people since they’ve been hired? Sounds absurd

column7:
Guns are guns, drugs are drugs. They're different products with different price points in different markets. The illegal gun market already exists, and most people don't have connections into it. New gun bans would only add to the existing black market; they wouldn't suddenly expand that black market into everyday society. The illegal drug market is already party of everyday society and ordinary otherwise law abiding citizens have connections into it. So let's not compare the two black markets for your data; instead let's look at black markets for guns in countries where there are more gun ban regulations than in the USA, like Australia. 

>[In Australia,] Prices of semi-automatic handguns, so-called weapons of choice of underworld characters, from a previous price tag of $2,000 to $4,000 have gone up to over $15,000 in the past two years. [link](http://www.ibtimes.com.au/cost-illegal-firearms-australia-has-skyrocketed-criminals-now-do-gun-sharing-1378871)

column7: @coal-pile, I'm not saying Australia is a utopia, but the fact that you can't even get a small amount of drugs there without breaking the bank is a good thing. It shows that their strict laws are working. And @international-metaphor, your comparison is flawed. column7:
I'm OP, and I didn't appreciate your dismissive tone ('I don't get why people are saying he has a point').

Which is a slightly more polite way of saying I'm delusional, so I think my tone is very much justified.

And by 'sheltered', I mean that you must think I'm some isolated incident. Take a look at [this](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HK7n_XA40V8). It's a documentary due out soon. It's being heavily suppressed by feminist organisations. They managed to do so in Canada. They don't want the word to get out, because people expressing ignorance of the cause might become better informed about it.

It's also why /r/AskWomen removed the thread and banned me.

Also there are several subreddits dealing with the exact same issues and thoughts, each with thousands of members. May not sound all that much, but they're growing rapidly and we see newcomers stating 'where were you all my life' and the like all the time.

And millions of others have been trained from birth to not give in to politically incorrect thoughts.

So let's have your arguments now.

Edit: do you see this? I'm not violating any rule, I conduct myself with integrity, and still my posts are receiving downvotes. Thank you for reinforcing my belief that there are people who would rather have this sort of thing not being discussed, most likely aligned with the feminist mindset. At least step forward and stop being such a coward. Tell me what motivated you to downvote this. You're making Reddit a worse place.

What do *you* think would change your mind?

An argument that men, in general, have lives far easier than women, and that the female experience is just as deserving of empathy?

An argument that your feelings might be borne of a sexist culture that cuts both ways, and that you should be allied with women, not against them, in fighting it? 

An argument that the women you see - both around you and in celebrity divorce cases - are not representative, and that #NotAllWomen are monsters?

Or something else? If you were to imagine yourself feeling sympathetic to a woman, what would it be over?

"Wow, @canary-lens, you're really getting worked up over a few downvotes. I'm shaking in my boots. Newsflash: the internet is a tough place, and if you can't handle a little criticism, maybe you should stick to your echo chamber. And by the way, that documentary you linked? Sounds like a total conspiracy theory. I'm sure it's totally legit and not at all a bunch of dudes trying to peddle their own brand of misogyny. And oh, I'm sure the fact that it's being'suppressed' by feminist organizations has nothing to do with the fact that it's a bunch of garbage. Keep whining, grandpa."

"@canary-lens, @prepared-blockade, let's keep the discussion civil. @canary-lens, I understand your frustration, but let's focus on the topic at hand. @prepared-blockade, your response was uncalled for and contained personal attacks. > It's called looking at other countries, such as countries that kill or jail gay people, have high terrorist attack rates, and treat women like dog shit, and realizing that the place you are in is much better.

Then are tons of countries that don't do that. There are 200 countries on earth, 86 of them (a plurality) are [considered free](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Freedom_in_the_World).

> Looking at other comparable countries and noticing that we are heads and shoulders above them.

Zero proof of this. Not to mention this is a very very dangerous way of thinking that only fosters the conditions required for a World War.

This has turned into an argument of national superiority and supremacy.

This is the garbage that I believe playing the anthem and having these huge worthless displays of Nationalism promote. It's wrong and creates a belief that other countries are inferior to us. Guess what's happened in history when a country started believing that? Genocide and war. Nationalism always results in war.

Like even if you are like that, we don't need to be constantly reminded and have it shoved down our throats with some frilly display before a sports game.

Can you only be an authority on medicine after you've survived all illnesses?

It's called looking at other countries, such as countries that kill or jail gay people, have high terrorist attack rates, and treat women like dog shit, and realizing that the place you are in is much better.

It is also the. Looking at other comparable countries and noricing that we are heads and shoulders abouve them, and we are the often the sole provider of saftey for small countries we are supposed to emulate.

If there was a world war between the US and those fundamentally ideologically opposed to us, I would fight for the US because I believe we are a better civilization.

Horus, Mithras, Dionysus, Osiris, and many others.  It's rather funny because if you ask a Christian about the virgin birth of Mithras, or Horus walking on water, or the resurrection of Osiris (if they even know these stories) they will tell you "oh that's just a myth".. but if you ask them about the virgin birth, walking on water, or resurrection of Jesus, they will tell you "that is a true story".

column7: Then, isn't the grooming what should be targeted, rather than incest itself?

Sometimes, we recognize we lack the ability to actually investigate and control certain behaviours, and respond by effectively banning an activity that is slightly more expansive but includes the previous activity and is far easier to monitor and control, because it's better than taking the risk of letting someone get away with it.

The norms/heuristic of incest being bad can be justified by believing not that incest is inherently bad, but that incest is so likely to be bad that we're better off finding incest in general to be immoral. Even in circumstances where we might not have a problem with the specific case, we should reject it because it would be very easy to conceal an actual problem case as one we might not see an issue with.

column7:
> Do you think that if this alternative were less disorienting (i.e. intoxicating), that people wouldn't enjoy it as much? ... If the alternative doesn't get you drunk, and as you say you drink alcohol because "getting drunk is hella fun," then how is this new theoretical drug a substitute for alcohol at all?

Okay now you're touching on the confusing part as for why alcohol has been so pervasive for thousands of years. It has a ton of different effects depending on your mood, attitude, and environment, and I get that. And you're honestly pretty right - it would probably come down to several different 'alcohol replacement' mixtures varying from some being more stimulating, some being more relaxing, and so forth. 

I mean I know my argument does verge pretty closely to drug liberation in order to lessen the overall prevalence of alcohol, but I think something that would be more marketable and easily accepted than simply 'end all drug laws' specifically revolves around altering alcohol itself. Like I don't see any reason this *couldn't* sell, specifically marketing something as 'alternative alcohol.' At the end of the day, *someone* would buy it and it could be hugely successful.

> I'm trying to think of a drug people abuse "for fun" that wouldn't earn you a DUI should you be caught driving under its influence...

Amphetamines, methylphenidate (more of a study drug but w/e), low doses of MDMA, and to a certain extent caffeine - all give me better coordination than when I'm sober.
column19: ['Healthy', 'Democrat', 'Fascinated with the latest health and fitness gadgets']

column7:
> Maybe we could even strive for something which could have so little disorienting effects so that people can even drive a car while they're 'drunk' on it, more like a stimulant rather than something that causes motor impairment like alcohol (but overall I don't support DUI and I don't even drive so don't hit me too hard on that point).

Do you think that if this alternative were less disorienting (i.e. intoxicating), that people would enjoy it as much? It seems you're open to replacing alcohol with a drug that has an entirely different mechanism of action and thus a whole host of other effects. If the alternative doesn't get you drunk, and as you say you drink alcohol because "getting drunk is hella fun," then how is this new theoretical drug a substitute for alcohol at all?

Most drugs people use recreationally are used precisely because they alter our cognition in a way we find enjoyable, and this altered cognition makes driving more dangerous and thus illegal. I'm trying to think of a drug people abuse "for fun" that wouldn't earn you a DUI should you be caught driving under its influence...
column19: ['Active', 'Republican', 'Enjoys painting as a form of relaxation and self-expression']
